mechanism_narrative: A persistent upward trend in industrial production signals expanding output capacity and aggregate demand, driving positive investor sentiment and higher S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Over any 2-year rolling window, when INDPRO 6-month change is positive, the median 20-day SPX return is negative or below the series median.

mechanism_narrative: When the federal funds rate surpasses 5.5%, the cost of capital becomes restrictive enough to compress corporate profit margins and reduce equity valuations, leading to negative S&P 500 returns over the subsequent 20 trading days.
failure_condition: After FEDFUNDS crosses above 5.5%, the average 20-day SPX return is not significantly negative (t-test p > 0.10) or the win rate exceeds 50%.

mechanism_narrative: A simultaneous rise in the unemployment rate above 4% and a decline in payroll employment over three consecutive months indicates a weakening labor market that reduces consumer spending and corporate earnings, depressing equity prices over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: In triggered instances, the 60-day SPX return is positive in more than 40% of cases or the average return is not statistically negative.

mechanism_narrative: During economic expansions when retail and food services sales are growing above the recent trend, robust consumer demand boosts corporate revenues and earnings, lifting equity prices over the next 5 trading days as positive momentum persists.
regime_hint: economic expansion with rising retail sales
failure_condition: When RSAFS 6-month change is positive, the 5-day SPX return is negative in more than half of the observations over any 3-year window.

mechanism_narrative: Housing starts exceeding 1.6 million units indicate strong residential investment and consumer confidence, which translates into higher economic output and positive equity returns over a 60-trading-day horizon.
failure_condition: After HOUST exceeds 1600, the median 60-day SPX return is not greater than the overall sample median.

mechanism_narrative: When the federal funds rate is above 5% and the unemployment rate is below 4%, the economy is in a late-cycle tight state where restrictive monetary policy combined with a taut labor market heightens recession risk, driving negative S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: In periods where both conditions hold, the 20-day SPX return is positive more than 40% of the time or the average return is not significantly negative at the 5% level.

mechanism_narrative: A sustained rise in the Consumer Price Index above trend signals persistent inflation that erodes real purchasing power and compels the central bank to maintain high interest rates, compressing equity valuations and yielding negative forward returns over 20 trading days.
failure_condition: When CPIAUCSL 12-month change is positive, the 20-day SPX return is negative less than 55% of the time over any 2-year window.

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate exceeds 4.5%, the economy is entering a contraction phase with rising joblessness that depresses consumer spending and corporate earnings, leading to negative S&P 500 returns over the subsequent 60 trading days.
failure_condition: After UNRATE crosses above 4.5%, the 60-day SPX return is positive in more than one-third of cases or the median return is not negative.
